noticeTemplate: This educational material references {work_title} by {authors}. All referenced content is used under Fair Use (17 U.S.C. § 107) for educational purposes. {additional_notes} We encourage you to purchase this work directly from {creator_link}.

additionalNotes.transformative: Content is transformed for interactive educational use, not reproduced verbatim. Skills are reframed as hands-on learning activities with full cultural context.
additionalNotes.amount: Only factual techniques, publicly shared cultural knowledge, and historically documented methods are referenced. Community-restricted and sacred content is never reproduced.
additionalNotes.marketEffect: This educational pack actively encourages purchase of the original works. Indigenous-owned publishers and bookstores are prioritized in all purchase links.
additionalNotes.purpose: Educational and non-commercial use for cultural preservation and heritage skills instruction, conducted with respect for First Nations data sovereignty principles.
additionalNotes.ocapCompliance: All use of First Nations knowledge in this pack is conducted in accordance with the OCAP Principles (Ownership, Control, Access, Possession) established by the First Nations Information Governance Centre. Only Level 1 (Publicly Shared) and Level 2 (Contextually Shared) knowledge is included. No Level 3 (Community Restricted) or Level 4 (Sacred/Ceremonial) content is reproduced under any circumstances.

creatorSupportStatement: The Heritage Skills Educational Pack directs all learners to purchase original works from their creators and to support Indigenous-owned publishers and bookstores. Purchasing from Indigenous publishers directly supports community-controlled cultural production and language revitalization.
culturalSovereigntyStatement: First Nations peoples retain full sovereignty over their traditional knowledge systems. The inclusion of any First Nations cultural knowledge in this educational pack does not constitute a claim of ownership, license, or right of reproduction beyond the specific educational use described here. Learners are strongly encouraged to seek direct relationships with First Nations communities, knowledge keepers, and cultural centers for deeper engagement with these traditions.
ocapComplianceNote: This pack acknowledges the OCAP® Principles (a registered trademark of the First Nations Information Governance Centre) as the governing framework for all First Nations knowledge included here. We support the principle that First Nations data and knowledge governance belongs to First Nations communities.
communityConsultationAcknowledgment: The selection and framing of First Nations knowledge in this educational pack was guided by published community-authorized resources, academic sources with documented community cooperation, and works authored by First Nations knowledge holders. We acknowledge that no set of published resources fully replaces direct relationship with First Nations communities, and we encourage learners to seek those direct connections.
